cases.belligerent: hostile and aggressive, ready to fight
cases.preemptive: taken as a measure against something anticipated, acting first to prevent something
cases.forestall: to prevent something by acting in advance
cases.ploy: a cunning plan or trick designed to gain an advantage
cases.misconstrue: to misinterpret someone's words or actions
cases.frugal: sparing with money or resources, economical
cases.congenial: pleasant and agreeable, suited to one's needs
cases.interloper: a person who intrudes into a place or situation without invitation
cases.chasm: a large, deep divide or gap, literal or figurative
cases.reconnaissance: military observation of a region to gather information
cases.belie: to give a false impression of something
cases.hedonistic: devoted to the pursuit of pleasure and self-indulgence
cases.grovel: to act with humility or servility to gain forgiveness or favor
cases.estranged: no longer close or affectionate, alienated
cases.discern: to perceive or recognize something clearly
cases.coercive: using force or threats to make someone do something
cases.obtuse: slow to understand, dim-witted, or annoyingly insensitive
cases.wallflower: a shy person who avoids attention, especially at a social event
cases.arson: the criminal act of deliberately setting fire to property
cases.indomitable: impossible to subdue or defeat, unyielding
cases.dilly-dally: to waste time through indecision or aimlessness
cases.reverie: a state of being pleasantly lost in one's thoughts, a daydream
cases.wanton: deliberate and unprovoked, or sexually unrestrained
cases.fiasco: a complete failure, usually in a humiliating way
cases.connoisseur: a person with expert knowledge and taste in a particular field
cases.necrophilia: sexual attraction to or intercourse with corpses
cases.poach: to lure away an employee or member from a rival organization
cases.frolic: to play and move about cheerfully and energetically
cases.plebeian: belonging to the common people, unrefined
cases.docile: ready to accept control or instruction, easily managed
cases.demented: suffering from severe mental impairment, mad
cases.ingratiate: to bring oneself into someone's favor by flattery
cases.outmaneuver: to gain an advantage over someone through skillful tactics
cases.subterfuge: deceit used to achieve a goal
cases.arcane: understood by few, mysterious and secret
cases.stifle: to suppress or hold back a feeling or reaction
cases.semblance: the outward appearance of something, especially when the reality is different
cases.pompous: excessively self-important or grand in manner
cases.platonic: a relationship that is affectionate but not romantic or sexual
cases.hypocrisy: claiming to have moral standards one does not actually hold
cases.penance: an act performed to show sorrow for wrongdoing
cases.cajole: to persuade someone through gentle flattery or coaxing
cases.linchpin: a person or thing vital to an enterprise, holding it together
cases.clandestine: kept secret, done in a concealed way
cases.inquisitive: curious, eager to learn or know things
cases.fervent: having intense feeling, passionate and earnest
cases.gloat: to show smug satisfaction about one's own success or another's misfortune
cases.preternatural: beyond what is normal, seemingly supernatural
cases.falter: to lose strength or momentum, to hesitate
cases.slink: to move somewhere quietly and secretively, especially from shame
cases.thwart: to prevent someone from accomplishing something
cases.standoffish: cold or distant in manner, unfriendly
cases.forbidding: having an unfriendly or threatening appearance
cases.collusion: secret cooperation for an illegal or deceitful purpose
cases.shrewd: having sharp judgment, especially in practical matters
